test.title: Test Project
test.short: A test project for demonstration purposes.
test.desc: This is a sample project description for testing and demonstration.

super-todo.title: Superb Todo List
super-todo.short: A complete task management app with priorities, deadlines, and dark mode.
super-todo.desc: A modern task management application built with Vue 3, going well beyond a simple list with a priority system, deadlines with visual alerts, dark mode, multilingual support, and a fully responsive interface.
super-todo.context: This project was created as part of a course on JavaScript frameworks. The goal was to discover Vue.js and its Composition API by building a practical, functional application. I chose a Todo List because it's a classic that allows exploring many concepts: state management, reactivity, data persistence, and user interactions.
super-todo.objective: Build a complete and modern task management application, going well beyond a simple list. The expected result had to include a priority system, deadlines with visual alerts, a dark mode, multilingual support, and a responsive mobile-friendly interface.
super-todo.approach: I adopted a modular architecture with Vue 3 and the Composition API (<script setup>). Each component has a single responsibility: input (TodoInput), display (TodoList), filters (TodoFilters), and statistics (TodoStats). Persistence is handled via LocalStorage with deep watchers, and the dark theme uses CSS variables for instant switching. All styling is in native CSS3, without any framework.
features: Priority management (high, medium, low) with smart sorting
Deadlines with red visual alert when overdue
Dark/light mode with user preference persistence
Internationalization (French/English) including priority badges
Inline editing of text and date directly in the list
Responsive interface with smooth animations (TransitionGroup)
super-todo.learnings: This project allowed me to deeply understand Vue 3 reactivity, particularly ref, computed, and watch with the deep option. I also learned to structure a front-end application in a modular way, handle client-side persistence, and implement an elegant theming system with CSS variables.

api-auth.title: API Auth Favorites
api-auth.short: A secure REST API with JWT authentication, bcrypt hashing, and protected routes.
api-auth.desc: A backend REST API built with Node.js and Express, focused on secure authentication. The project implements a full JWT cycle, secure password storage with bcrypt, route-protection middleware, and a lightweight SQLite database - all with a built-in browser test interface.
api-auth.context: This project grew out of curiosity and experience gained during my apprenticeship - I had encountered authentication concepts at work (JWT, bcrypt, middlewares) without having had the chance to implement them from scratch myself. I decided to build this API to truly understand these mechanisms, with no coursework involved.
api-auth.objective: Understand and master the full server-side authentication cycle: registration, login, JWT generation and verification, route protection via middleware, and secure password storage. The result had to be a functional, testable, and cleanly structured API.
api-auth.approach: The architecture follows a clear separation of concerns: routes, controllers, middlewares, and the database are isolated. Passwords are hashed with bcryptjs (salt + hash), JWT tokens expire after 24 hours and are verified by a middleware injected on protected routes. Helmet secures HTTP headers. An HTML/JS interface is served at the root to test the API without any external tools.
features: Registration with bcrypt password hashing (salt + hash)
Login with signed JWT token generation (24h TTL)
Protected route /api/users/profile with Bearer token verification
Reusable and injectable authentication middleware
Lightweight serverless SQLite database
Built-in test interface accessible directly in the browser
HTTP header security with Helmet
api-auth.learnings: This project allowed me to truly grasp how JWT authentication works end-to-end: generation, transmission in headers, middleware verification. I also understood why bcrypt is essential (salt prevents rainbow table attacks) and how to cleanly structure a Node.js API with a proper routes / controllers / middlewares separation.

bike.title: ReCycle E-Bikes
bike.short: UI/UX mockup for a fictional eco-friendly electric bike brand showcase website.
bike.desc: Mockup designed in Figma for ReCycle E-Bikes, a fictional eco-friendly electric bike brand. The project includes an immersive hero, impact sections, product cards and a testimonials carousel, all with animations designed from the conception stage.
bike.context: This project was completed as part of an individual UI/UX mockup exercise, based on a client brief focused on 'eco-friendly electric bikes'. The client wanted a modern website highlighting the positive ecological impact, riding comfort and e-bike innovation, while staying consistent with visual references provided in Figma. The goal was to design a complete and convincing homepage for ReCycle E-Bikes, respecting the provided assets and incorporating a real reflection on animations and content presentation.
bike.objective: The expected deliverable was a desktop mockup for a showcase website structured around several key blocks: a strong hero with the message 'Revolutionize Your Commute with ReCycle E-Bikes', an environmental and economic impact section, technical and sustainable arguments, a showcase of bike models (EcoBoost, Nature Ride, Urban Leaf), and a customer testimonials block.
bike.approach: The mockup was designed in Figma, working on the overall composition (hero, impact, models, testimonials), art direction (sober yet modern palette, readable typography), and planned animations materialized through variants: header animation, hover effects on cards, transitions on models and the carousel. The goal was purely UI/UX: thinking about motion design at the mockup level, without writing any code.
features: Immersive hero with 'Revolutionize Your Commute' message and dual call-to-action
Environmental & economic impact section with key figures
Technical arguments (premium materials, advanced technology, sustainable manufacturing)
Showcase of 3 bike models (EcoBoost, Nature Ride, Urban Leaf) as product cards
Customer testimonials carousel with navigation
Planned animations (hover, reveal, transitions) materialized as Figma variants
bike.learnings: On this project, I learned to follow a brief while maintaining a strong identity, to work on animations from the mockup stage (imagining header, cards and carousel behavior as variants), to structure a conversion-oriented landing page (hook, benefits, proof, CTA), and to highlight brand values in a clear and credible way.

lkk.title: Lee Kum Kee - Redesign
lkk.short: Group project redesigning the Lee Kum Kee website with art direction and animations.
lkk.desc: Group project redesigning the Lee Kum Kee website (Asian sauces). The Figma mockup proposes a more modern, readable and appetizing version of the site, with product sections, recipes, newsletter and micro-interactions designed at the design level.
lkk.context: This project was carried out as part of a group assignment, with a client brief centered on redesigning the Lee Kum Kee website (Asian sauces). The client gave relative creative freedom on style, as long as we respected the general structure (about us, products, recipes, newsletter) and used product/food visuals. The goal was to propose a more modern and appetizing version of the site, while learning to create a professional mockup as a team.
lkk.objective: Deliver a complete showcase website mockup, featuring a strong homepage with the tagline 'Where True Flavor Begins', Products, About Us, Recipes and Newsletter sections, with appetizing visuals and a professional footer. The idea was to produce a realistic design that could serve as a foundation for a website redesign: coherent navigation, readable hierarchy, respected brand tone.
lkk.approach: The mockup was created in Figma, working on layout, typography, color scheme and planned animations. We respected the brief's sections, set up product blocks with visuals and CTAs, developed a brand-inspired color palette (deep reds, golds, cream tones) with a detailed color guide, and designed micro-interactions (sauce reveals, recipe card hovers, newsletter pop-in).
features: Homepage with 'Where True Flavor Begins' hero and featured products
'About Us' section with sub-sections: Roots, Quality, Sustainability
Recipe catalog with dish thumbnails and appetizing descriptions
Newsletter block with email field, CTA and GDPR notice
Micro-interactions and animations designed at the design level (hover, pop-in, transitions)
Professional footer with full Lee Kum Kee Europe contact information
lkk.learnings: On this project, I learned to work from a client brief while proposing a credible creative direction, to collaborate as a group on a mockup (splitting pages, maintaining style consistency), to design animations at the design level (hover, transitions...) and to structure a coherent brand experience through content and visuals.

rl-wp.title: Rocket League Fan Site
rl-wp.short: Responsive mockup of a Rocket League fan site, designed for WordPress integration.
rl-wp.desc: Responsive Figma mockup (desktop + mobile) for an unofficial Rocket League fan site, designed for future WordPress integration. The design includes an immersive homepage, a humorous patch notes blog, a contact page and a structure designed as WordPress templates.
rl-wp.context: This project was completed as part of a WordPress integration course at My Digital School in Grenoble. As a passionate Rocket League player, I chose this theme to make the exercise more personal: rather than a generic website, I designed an unofficial fan site dedicated to the game, with its codes, memes and esports culture. The idea was to create a site I would genuinely enjoy visiting as a player.
rl-wp.objective: Produce a complete responsive mockup, ready to be integrated into a WordPress theme: an immersive homepage featuring Season 19, a World Championship 2025 section, an editorial section on the game's history, a humorous patch notes blog, and a contact page with form. The result had to be visually coherent and structured for transposition into WordPress templates.
rl-wp.approach: The mockup was entirely created in Figma, in both desktop and mobile versions, without animations, with particular attention to visual hierarchy and future WordPress integration. I broke down the site into reusable sections (hero, news blocks, articles, contact, footer), worked on a responsive grid, and set up components (buttons, article cards, patch notes blocks). The mockup anticipates WordPress integration with page, blog and contact templates.
features: Immersive homepage with humorous tagline and Season 19 showcase
World Championship 2025 section at the LOLC Arena in Lyon
'What is Rocket League?' section with the game's history from SARPBC
Humorous patch notes blog with the game's community tone
Contact page with form and info (username, social media)
Desktop and mobile responsive design, prepared for WordPress integration
rl-wp.learnings: This project helped me improve on following a brief and structuring a project, designing an 'integrable' mockup (sections, reusable components, responsive from the design stage), working on a design centered on a video game universe (humor and readability), and preparing for WordPress integration (templates, dynamic vs static content, form, footer).
